target.title: feat(telegram): add acknowledgement sticker support
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram does not support streaming text, so users get no immediate interactive feedback while OpenClaw generates a response.
- Why it matters: Ack reactions help, but stickers are more visible and expressive for Telegram conversations.
- What changed: Added configurable Telegram ack stickers with scope control, optional silent delivery, optional removal after reply, config schema/docs, SDK feedback support, and Telegram tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Existing ack reaction behavior remains supported; this does not add text streaming to Telegram or change default Telegram access policy.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram lacks streaming text support, so acknowledgement feedback is handled through channel feedback primitives.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/identity.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ack-sticker.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Telegram resolves ackSticker config, sends the configured sticker before dispatching the agent turn, and optionally removes it after reply.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers config resolution, channel feedback shape, Telegram send behavior, and dispatch cleanup without requiring live Telegram credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing ack reaction tests covered reaction behavior only.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram users can configure `ackSticker` globally, per account, per direct/group chat, or per topic. When enabled, OpenClaw sends a Telegram sticker acknowledgement before generating the response. Existing ack reactions continue to work.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Telegram message -> OpenClaw agent loop -> Telegram final reply

After:
Telegram message -> ack sticker -> OpenClaw agent loop -> Telegram final reply -> optional ack sticker cleanup
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Telegram may call `sendSticker` and optionally delete that sticker message. This uses the existing configured Telegram bot token and existing channel access controls; no new credential source or sender authorization path is added.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm
- Model/provider: Not required for unit coverage
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  channels: {
    telegram: {
      enabled: true,
      ackSticker: {
        fileId: "CAAC...",
        scope: "direct",
        silent: true,
        removeAfterReply: true,
      },
    },
  },
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure Telegram `ackSticker.fileId` with a sendable Telegram sticker file id.
2. Start the OpenClaw gateway with Telegram enabled.
3. Send a DM to the Telegram bot.

### Expected

- Telegram sends the configured acknowledgement sticker before the agent response.
- The agent response is delivered normally.
- If `removeAfterReply` is enabled, the acknowledgement sticker is deleted after the final reply.

### Actual

- Unit and config tests pass for the implemented behavior.
- Live Telegram verification was attempted locally but blocked by local gateway token/env setup issues, not by the ackSticker implementation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation run:

```sh
pnpm test src/agents/identity.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ack-sticker.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:docs:gen
pnpm config:docs:check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed-ts-files>
git diff --check
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Config schema accepts ackSticker fields.
  - Channel feedback carries ackSticker configuration.
  - Telegram send path sends stickers and respects notification options.
  - Telegram dispatch path can clean up ack stickers after reply.
  - Docs/generated config baseline are updated.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Scope resolution mirrors ack reaction behavior.
  - Sticker cleanup is best-effort.
  - Ack reaction remains available and can be disabled independently.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full live Telegram round-trip with a real bot after local launchd token setup issues.
  - Broad full-suite CI locally.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Existing configs continue to work. Users can opt in by adding `channels.telegram.ackSticker` or `messages.ackSticker`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Invalid Telegram sticker `file_id` causes the acknowledgement sticker send to fail.
  - Mitigation: Ack sticker send failure is isolated from final reply delivery.
- Risk: Sticker deletion can fail due to Telegram permissions or timing.
  - Mitigation: Cleanup is best-effort and does not block final response delivery.

target.title: fix(memory, builtin backend): eagerly preload session transcript listeners at gateway startup
target.body:
## Summary (scope narrowed to builtin backend per Codex review)

Eagerly preload `MemoryIndexManager` at gateway startup for every agent whose `agents.defaults.memorySearch.sources` (or per-agent overlay) includes `"sessions"`, **when using the builtin session-file backend**. This arms `ensureSessionListener()` before the first `sessionTranscriptUpdate` event fires, so `/reset` and `/new` archive emits actually land in the listener set and get indexed into `chunks` without the user having to run `openclaw memory index --force`.

## Why it was broken

`MemoryIndexManager` is lazy-loaded on the first `memory_search` tool call. If a user types `/reset` or `/new` before that first search, the builtin `archiveFileOnDisk` emit lands in an empty `SESSION_TRANSCRIPT_LISTENERS` set and is silently dropped, so the resulting `.jsonl.reset.<iso>` / `.jsonl.deleted.<iso>` archives never enter `chunks`. Only `memory index --force` recovers them (which is #76311 / #76452's escape hatch but is not a cold-start fix).

## Why builtin-only (Codex review response)

Thanks @clawsweeper for the P2 catch on the qmd path. The initial revision of this PR treated any successful `getActiveMemorySearchManager` result as listener-armed, but under the qmd backend that call returns the `FallbackMemoryManager` wrapper whose inner `MemoryIndexManager` is only constructed by `fallbackFactory`. So on qmd, merely obtaining the wrapper does NOT run `ensureSessionListener()`, and the cold-start race still drops `sessionTranscriptUpdate` emits.

Three options were on the table (per Codex):
1. Explicitly preload the builtin `MemoryIndexManager` listener owner even for qmd — requires reaching into the FallbackMemoryManager fallback slot.
2. Add qmd-native transcript event handling inside `qmd-manager.ts`.
3. **Narrow this PR to builtin-only and defer qmd to a follow-up.**

This revision takes **option 3**: added an explicit early-return guard `if (params.cfg.memory?.backend === "qmd") { ... return; }` at the top of `startGatewayMemorySessionListeners`, with a log line making the scope decision visible, plus a regression test (`skips preload entirely when memory.backend is qmd (Codex review scope guard)`). The changelog entry is also retitled `Memory/sessions (builtin backend):` and calls out the intentional qmd deferral.

## Independent verification (builtin only)

CarHer builtin-backend deployment (`carher-199` baseline). Three cold restarts, same container, identical `/reset` sequence:

| | pre-preload | post-preload |
|---|---|---|
| archive emit lands in listener set | ❌ dropped | ✅ captured |
| `chunks` delta after 30s | +0 | +41 |
| verified commit | `main @ 9a49d143c819` | `HEAD of this PR` |

## What is NOT included

- qmd backend session-listener arming (follow-up PR, tracked in body + changelog).
- Changes to `qmd-manager.ts` transcript event handling.
- Changes to `FallbackMemoryManager` fallback construction.

Refs #56131. AI-assisted.

target.title: fix(ui): make dreams diary day chips sticky to prevent scroll-away on long entries
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #76638 — when a diary entry had long content (10+ paragraphs), the day-chip navigation bar at the top would be pushed out of the viewport, making it impossible to switch between dates.

## Root cause

`.dreams-diary__daychips` used `position: relative` with no sticky behavior. Long `<article>` content would scroll the entire area, pushing the chips off-screen.

## Fix

Changed `.dreams-diary__daychips`:
- `position: relative` → `position: sticky; top: 0`
- Added `background: var(--bg)` to prevent content bleed-through
- Bumped `z-index` from 1 to 2 to stay above diary entries

## Test plan

- [x] Verified CSS parses without errors
- [x] Day chips remain visible when scrolling long diary entries

target.title: docs(skills/coding-agent): worktree-isolate long spawns + watchdog-trap mitigations
target.body:
## Summary

Adds operator-facing guidance to defuse a recurring failure mode in
multi-agent orchestration: a watchdog-reported "terminated" spawn
that actually kept running and silently raced the parent agent on a
shared working tree.

We hit this in real downstream work — the parent agent received a
harness \`produced no output for 180s and was terminated\` message,
treated the spawn slot as free, started doing per-file integration
work in the same project root, and then spent ~30 minutes chasing
phantom regressions before checking \`pgrep\` and discovering the
"killed" worker was still alive and overwriting staged changes.

This PR is the operator-side workaround. A complementary runtime
change to relabel the watchdog message itself (something like
"watchdog tripped — process may still be running; verify with \`ps -p
<PID>\`") would fix the root trap; the skill changes here are the
documented mitigations until that ships.

## Changes (all in \`skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md\`)

1. **Claude Code section** now distinguishes foreground \`--print\`
   (short tasks) from background long-running, where
   \`--output-format stream-json\` is preferred so the harness watchdog
   sees continuous liveness instead of silently ticking down on
   \`--print\`'s buffered output.

2. **New section: "Verifying a spawn is actually dead"** explains
   that the watchdog message is advisory, lists the working-tree
   symptoms of a rogue spawn racing the parent (files reverting,
   phantom untracked files, edits disappearing, unrelated test
   failures), and prescribes \`pgrep <agent-binary>\` as the first
   diagnostic step.

3. **Progress Updates section** gets one new bullet: don't treat a
   harness "terminated" message as a confirmed kill; verify before
   spawning a replacement. Points readers at the new section.

4. **Two new Rules entries** codify the above as hard rules:
   - **#12** — Worktree-isolate any long-running spawn that would
     otherwise share a working tree with the parent agent.
   - **#13** — Watchdog "terminated" messages are advisory, not
     authoritative. Verify with \`ps -p\` / \`pgrep\`. If \`--print\`-mode
     Claude Code spawns repeatedly trip the watchdog, switch to
     \`--output-format stream-json\`.

## Why these specifically

- \`--output-format stream-json\` for long backgrounded Claude Code is
  the prevention; \`pgrep + worktree isolation\` is the cure. Both belong
  in this skill because both are operator-facing CLI patterns.
- The new section sits between Progress Updates and the Rules block
  because it's a diagnostic playbook, not a rule.
- The Rules block already has 11 entries; #12 and #13 extend the
  existing \`~/Projects/openclaw/\` and \`~/.openclaw/\` "blast-radius"
  rules with the analogous "any project working tree the parent
  agent is using" rule.

## Test plan

- [ ] Read the diff (62 lines added, 1 deleted; one file).
- [ ] Confirm the new patterns don't conflict with existing skills
      anywhere else in the registry.
- [ ] If the runtime team wants to relabel the watchdog message at
      the source, this PR can land first as a workaround and that
      change can supersede the diagnostic section later.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix: ensure CLI processes exit after command completion on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes CLI processes (node.exe openclaw.mjs) remaining alive after command execution on Windows. The CLI entrypoint was missing process.exit() calls after commands completed, causing processes to hang.

## Changes

- Added explicit `process.exit(process.exitCode ?? 0)` call after CLI entrypoint execution in openclaw.mjs
- Ensures all async code paths properly terminate on Windows

## Testing

- Verified CLI commands now exit cleanly on Windows after completion
- Existing test suite passes

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#74378

commands.review_summary:
Adds a Microsoft ecosystem tracker markdown file, a GitHub CLI generator that can refresh it or update a PR body, and a root changelog entry.

PR surface: Docs +422, Other +428. Total +850 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection plus live GitHub metadata show raw titles are copied into generated body text, three closing references are active, and a WindowServer false positive remains in the current tracker.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed because this protected draft PR is conflicting, missing contributor proof, and needs a decision on whether the tracker belongs as committed repo surface or PR-body-only metadata.

target.title: WORKING: All Microsoft Issues and PRs (refresh)
target.body:
# Microsoft Ecosystem Issues & PRs Tracker

> **Purpose:** Living checklist for maintainers to track all open Microsoft-related issues and PRs (Teams, Windows, WSL, Azure, M365/SharePoint).
>
> **How to use:**
>
> - Mark items resolved by editing this PR body and changing `[ ]` to `[x]`
> - Claim items by adding your GitHub handle to the `Assignee` column
> - Priority guide: **P0** = crash/blocker/security, **P1** = significant bug/regression, **P2** = minor bug/enhancement, **P3** = nice-to-have/stale
> - Items marked _(stale)_ have been flagged by the stale bot due to inactivity
>
> **Last updated:** 2026-06-28 (post-purge audit: refreshed from currently open GitHub issues/PRs and rebuilt from PR #49126 format)

---

## Summary

| Category | Issues | PRs | Total | Closed | Remaining |
| -------- | ------ | --- | ----- | ------ | --------- |
| MS Teams (channel plugin) | 11 | 44 | 55 | 0 | 55 |
| Windows platform | 51 | 42 | 93 | 0 | 93 |
| WSL | 9 | 1 | 10 | 0 | 10 |
| Azure | 8 | 4 | 12 | 0 | 12 |
| SharePoint / M365 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| **Total** | **79** | **91** | **170** | **0** | **170** |

---

target.title: fix(ios): stabilize chat streaming layout and session flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: iOS chat streaming could attach to a synthetic placeholder lane, churn during initial history/bootstrap, and reparse streaming content in ways that caused flicker or session drift.
- Why it matters: active conversations can show unstable streaming UI, lose the correct lane/session association, or visibly flicker while tokens arrive.
- What changed: scoped streaming to the active session key, hardened stream acceptance/initial load behavior, and stabilized layout parsing/visibility updates.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no new model behavior. This fix lives in the shared `OpenClawChatUI` layer used by both iOS and macOS. The bug (cross-run UI drift during concurrent session-key agent events) affects any client using `ChatViewModel`; macOS uses the same chat view and benefits equally, but the fix is not platform-specific — it guards shared streaming state.
- AI-assisted: yes (AI-assisted implementation, contributor-reviewed).
- Testing level: targeted shared-package tests plus iOS build validation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #40985
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: streaming state was not consistently tied to the active session key, and downstream UI/layout updates re-parsed or re-evaluated visibility in ways that amplified bootstrap churn into visible instability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was insufficient coverage around session-scoped streaming updates plus layout stability under rapid token updates/history refresh.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): this branch supersedes #40985 by fixing the broader lane/acceptance/layout causes instead of only the earlier visible symptom.
- Why this regressed now: incremental fixes addressed isolated symptoms while the newer streaming lane introduced more state transitions that needed session-scoped and layout-stable handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: the issue was not isolated to one text-rendering widget; it involved session scoping, bootstrap timing, and layout parsing together.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/AssistantTextParserTests.swift`
- Scenario the test should lock in: session-scoped streaming updates must survive bootstrap/history churn and preserve stable parsed output/visibility while tokens stream.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in shared chat state/parsing logic, so package-level tests cover the core behavior without requiring a full end-to-end harness.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): targeted parser/view-model tests were added/expanded for this branch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- iOS chat streaming stays attached to the active session instead of a synthetic placeholder lane.
- Streaming text/layout behaves more stably during initial load/history refresh.
- Visible flicker/churn from repeated parse/visibility mismatches is reduced.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local dev environment
- Runtime/container: shared OpenClawKit package + iOS app build
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): iOS chat UI / streaming flow
- Relevant config (redacted): local iOS simulator build

### Steps

1. Build the shared package and iOS app from this branch.
2. Open a chat session and trigger a streaming reply.
3. Observe initial history/bootstrap plus active token streaming.

### Expected

- Streaming binds to the active session.
- Bootstrap/history churn does not destabilize the active stream.
- Layout remains visually stable while tokens arrive.

### Actual

- Matched expected in targeted tests and local build validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests`
  - `xcodegen generate`
  - `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build`
- Edge cases checked:
  - stream acceptance during bootstrap/history load
  - parser stability under repeated streaming updates
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full repository CI matrix
  - non-iOS clients

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - All 19 review threads (P1/P2 from `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]` + `copilot-pull-request-reviewer`) resolved in this update.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A



## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: chat-state timing bugs can reappear under bootstrapping or external-final sync edge cases.
  - Mitigation: this patch scopes state to the active session and adds targeted parser/view-model coverage around the churn paths.



## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: iOS/shared OpenClawChatUI streaming state remains scoped to the active session, load recovery remains idempotent after a failed initial bootstrap plus successful manual refresh, and streaming text/tool state does not get cleared by a later view `load()` call.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local dev environment, Xcode 26.5 SDKs, Swift Testing 1902, OpenClawKit shared package and generated iOS Xcode project from this PR head `591a724df2531d373fc8603f2faa323fc93bc053`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml
xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawChatUI -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
ChatViewModelTests: Test run with 83 tests in 1 suite passed after 5.550 seconds.
AssistantTextParserTests: Test run with 7 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.001 seconds.
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml: Created project at /Users/eulices/projects/openclaw-pr50483/apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj
xcodebuild OpenClawChatUI generic iOS Simulator: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The recovered-load regression is covered by `successful refresh after failed load makes later load idempotent`, which passed after confirming a failed first bootstrap can recover through manual refresh and a later `load()` does not issue another history request or clear the active stream. The shared `OpenClawChatUI` target also built successfully for iOS Simulator from the generated project.
- **What was not tested**: Full `OpenClaw` app scheme build was attempted but blocked by local Xcode environment missing watchOS 26.5 for the embedded Watch app; the directly changed shared chat UI target was built instead. Full repository CI matrix was left to GitHub.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime / source tree
- Model/provider: N/A for core session-switch logic
- Integration/channel (if any): chat-channel command flow; manually validated in a real chat session
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `session.historyLimit: 5` (default path)
  - `session.historyLimit: 0` (disable-history regression path)

### Steps

1. Start a chat conversation and create multiple sessions with `/new`.
2. Run `/sessions`, then switch via `/session <number>` or `/session back`.
3. Continue creating sessions until the history limit is exceeded.

### Expected

- `/sessions` lists current + recent switchable sessions in LRU order.
- Switching restores the target session’s stored metadata/snapshots.
- Sessions still present in history remain switchable.
- The oldest session is archived only after LRU eviction.
- With `session.historyLimit=0`, switchable history is disabled and the previous session is archived immediately on reset.

### Actual

- Behavior matches the expected results above in targeted tests and manual validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `/sessions` output in chat
  - `/session back` restores the previous session
  - switching to a still-retained historical session restores expected context/state
  - LRU eviction archives only the oldest evicted session
  - daily reset path preserves switchable history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` disables switchable history and archives immediately on reset
- Edge cases checked:
  - history boundary behavior at the configured limit
  - selecting current session is a no-op
  - unique-prefix SessionId selection behavior
  - stale/daily-reset session rollover
- What you did **not** verify:
  - every individual chat provider/plugin surface
  - cross-SessionKey switching (out of scope)
  - any UI/card-based session picker (out of scope)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - revert this PR, or set `session.historyLimit=0` to disable switchable session history
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`
  - `src/config/sessions/types.ts`
  - `src/config/types.base.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - `/sessions` shows stale/incorrect ordering
  - switched sessions lose expected token/context/compaction snapshot state
  - transcripts are archived too early while still present in history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` does not disable switchable history

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: LRU eviction/archive timing could regress and archive a session that should still remain switchable.
  - Mitigation: Added/updated targeted tests covering retained-history vs evicted-history behavior.

- Risk: Snapshot restore could leave token/context/compaction state inconsistent after switching.
  - Mitigation: Added lifecycle tests covering restore and missing-metadata fallback behavior.

- Risk: Docs could drift from implementation again as follow-up fixes land.
  - Mitigation: Updated `docs/design/chat-session-switch.md` to match the current implementation semantics in this PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: Yes

I used an AI assistant for code review, doc cleanup, and targeted test updates. I manually reviewed the final diff, understood the changes, and verified the session-switch flows myself.

target.title: fix(models): mark local Ollama rows available
target.body:
Closes #92224

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running `openclaw models list --json` could see a reachable local Ollama model reported with `local: true` but `available: false` when registry model-level availability did not include that exact model key.

## Why This Change Was Made

Local model rows already classify loopback/local `baseUrl` values, and the models CLI documents availability/auth as provider-level metadata from local-provider markers rather than a live per-model execution probe. This change treats local provider rows as available from that local marker while preserving registry model-key availability and provider-auth fallback for non-local rows.

## User Impact

Users with local Ollama models now get model-list output that matches the local-provider auth semantics: configured local Ollama rows no longer look unavailable solely because registry discovery omitted the exact model key.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: a reachable local Ollama model is listed with `local: true` and `available: true` on the patched branch.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `docker run -d --rm --name openclaw-ollama-proof -p 11434:11434 alpine/ollama:latest` -> started a real Ollama daemon reachable at `http://127.0.0.1:11434`.
  - `docker exec openclaw-ollama-proof ollama pull smollm2:135m` -> pulled a local model into that daemon.
  - `curl.exe -sS http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags` -> returned `smollm2:135m` from the daemon.
  - `node --import tsx src/entry.ts models list --json --provider ollama` at commit `b5390f5c15`, with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and `OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama-local` -> passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.model-row.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, 4 tests.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.rows.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, 4 tests.
  - `git diff --check` -> passed.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Observed result: the new regression test fails before the fix with `expected false to be true`, then passes after local base URL rows are included in row availability.
- Real Ollama CLI proof output:

```text
COMMAND: node --import tsx src/entry.ts models list --json --provider ollama
COMMIT: b5390f5c15
OLLAMA_DAEMON: Docker Desktop container openclaw-ollama-proof, alpine/ollama:latest, reachable at http://127.0.0.1:11434
OPENCLAW_HOME: isolated temp dir
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH: isolated temp openclaw.json
OLLAMA_API_KEY: ollama-local
OLLAMA_TAGS_BEFORE: {"models":[{"name":"smollm2:135m","model":"smollm2:135m","modified_at":"2026-06-28T13:57:38.596605328Z","size":270898672,"digest":"9077fe9d2ae1a4a41a868836b56b8163731a8fe16621397028c2c76f838c6907","details":{"format":"gguf","family":"llama","families":["llama"],"parameter_size":"134.52M","quantization_level":"F16","context_length":8192,"embedding_length":576},"capabilities":["completion"]}]}
EXIT: 0
```

```json
{
  "count": 1,
  "models": [
    {
      "key": "ollama/smollm2:135m",
      "name": "smollm2:135m",
      "input": "text",
      "contextWindow": 8192,
      "local": true,
      "available": true,
      "tags": [],
      "missing": false
    }
  ]
}
```

target.title: fix(exec): decode Windows command output with codepage-aware streaming
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix Windows exec output mojibake for CJK locales by decoding captured command output with the active Windows code page instead of assuming UTF-8.
- Preserve decoder state across stdout/stderr chunks or decode buffered byte streams so split GBK/Shift_JIS characters are not replaced.
- Add focused regression coverage for CP936/GBK Chinese output and split multibyte chunks.

## Credit
This carries forward the useful approach from @slepybear's source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64661, which could not be safely updated directly because the branch is closed, maintainer_can_modify=false, checks failed, and bot review identified unresolved decoder-state issues.

## Linked Issues
Fixes/addresses the Windows CJK exec mojibake family reported in #40613, #50519, and #56462.

## Validation
- pnpm -s vitest run src/infra/windows-encoding.test.ts src/process/exec.windows.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed

## Preflight Note
Hydrate the mentioned #72393 before opening a replacement PR. If #72393 is already a live maintainer-owned replacement with equivalent coverage and clean review gates, route this cluster to that PR instead of creating a duplicate.

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156777-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64661
- Credit: Credit @slepybear and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64661 for the original Windows codepage-aware decoding approach.; Mention that #64661 could not be safely updated because it is closed, maintainer_can_modify=false, had relevant failing checks, and had actionable bot-review findings.; Preserve reporter context from #40613, #50519, and #56462 in the PR body.

target.title: fix(webchat): slash menu clipped by overflow and missing keyboard scroll
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The webchat slash command menu (`/`) is completely invisible — typing `/` changes the input border color and arrow-key selection works, but the dropdown never appears. This is because `.agent-chat__input` uses `overflow: hidden` (for textarea scroll containment), which clips the absolutely-positioned slash menu that renders above the input via `position: absolute; bottom: 100%`.
- Additionally, when the slash menu has more items than the visible scroll area, keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/ArrowDown) does not auto-scroll the active item into view — users must manually scroll with the mouse.
- What changed: Introduced `.agent-chat__input-wrap` as a positioning parent for the slash menu, so it renders outside the `overflow: hidden` container. Added `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` after each arrow-key index change.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No modifications to slash command logic, command execution, or any non-UI code.

![slash-menu-fix](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9623f14a-9a7d-43a8-82fe-f75d5be85ba2)
- Here above demonstrates the fix.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Slash command menu now appears above the input box when typing `/` in webchat.
- Arrow-key navigation in the slash menu auto-scrolls to keep the active item visible.
- No changes to existing keyboard shortcuts (Tab to fill, Enter to select, Escape to close).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.2.0)
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22+
- Integration/channel: Webchat (Control UI)

### Steps

1. Start gateway: `openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force`
2. Open `http://127.0.0.1:18789/webchat/`
3. Type `/` in the chat input

### Expected

- Slash command menu appears above the input box
- ArrowDown/ArrowUp navigates through commands with auto-scroll

### Actual (before fix)

- Input border changes color (`:focus-within` fires), arrow keys + Enter work (DOM exists), but the menu is completely invisible — clipped by `overflow: hidden`
- When menu is made visible via other means, keyboard navigation past visible items requires manual mouse scrolling

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording

Before fix — typing `/` shows no menu (only border color change):
The DOM is present and functional (keyboard selection works), but the popup is clipped to zero height by `overflow: hidden` on the parent.

After fix — slash menu visible and keyboard-scrollable:
Menu appears above input; ArrowDown past visible items auto-scrolls the list.

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios:
  - Built UI locally via `pnpm ui:build`, started gateway with `node openclaw.mjs gateway run`
  - Confirmed slash menu appears when typing `/`
  - Confirmed ArrowDown/ArrowUp auto-scrolls active item into view
  - Confirmed Tab fills command, Enter selects, Escape closes
  - Confirmed input box border-radius, textarea scroll, and backdrop-filter unaffected
  - Confirmed mobile layout selector updated (`.agent-chat__input-wrap` in `layout.mobile.css`)
- What I did **not** verify:
  - macOS/iOS native app webchat (only tested browser webchat)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the `.agent-chat__input-wrap` wrapper — move `margin`/`flex-shrink` back to `.agent-chat__input` and remove the wrapper `<div>` in `chat.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: If a CSS rule targets `.agent-chat__input` for margin/positioning, it may need updating to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`.

## Files changed

- `ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css` — Added `.agent-chat__input-wrap` class; moved `margin` and `flex-shrink` from `.agent-chat__input` to the wrapper
- `ui/src/styles/layout.mobile.css` — Updated mobile override selector from `.agent-chat__input` to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`
- `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` — Wrapped input bar in `.agent-chat__input-wrap`; moved `renderSlashMenu` outside `.agent-chat__input`; added `scrollSlashMenuActiveIntoView()` helper called after ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation

**AI-assisted:** Built with Claude Code. Tested manually on local gateway webchat.

target.title: fix(anthropic): accept Claude CLI apiKeyHelper auth
target.body:
﻿Fixes #97489

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running Anthropic models through the Claude CLI backend would hit `missing-provider-auth` before `claude` was spawned when their Claude Code auth came from `~/.claude/settings.json` `apiKeyHelper` instead of macOS Keychain or `~/.claude/.credentials.json`.

## Why This Change Was Made

Claude Code documents `apiKeyHelper` as a supported CLI credential source for dynamic credentials, so OpenClaw now treats a non-empty `apiKeyHelper` setting as Claude CLI-managed auth evidence. The auth gate uses a non-secret synthetic marker for `claude-cli` only; it does not execute the helper, persist helper output, or expose the marker through generic Anthropic API auth profiles.

## User Impact

Users with proxy, vault, or rotating-key Claude CLI setups can start OpenClaw turns through `agentRuntime.id: "claude-cli"` without creating a dummy `.credentials.json` file just to satisfy OpenClaw's pre-spawn auth check.

## Evidence

- Source contract checked: Claude Code docs list `apiKeyHelper` as a custom credential script and include it in authentication precedence: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/iam#credential-management
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/anthropic/cli-constants.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-migration.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts extensions/anthropic/index.test.ts extensions/anthropic/provider-discovery.ts extensions/anthropic/register.runtime.ts src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/commands/doctor-claude-cli.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic/cli-constants.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-migration.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts extensions/anthropic/index.test.ts extensions/anthropic/provider-discovery.ts extensions/anthropic/register.runtime.ts src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/commands/doctor-claude-cli.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx -e "void (async () => { await import('./src/agents/cli-credentials.ts'); await import('./extensions/anthropic/register.runtime.ts'); await import('./extensions/anthropic/provider-discovery.ts'); await import('./extensions/anthropic/cli-migration.ts'); await import('./src/commands/doctor-claude-cli.ts'); console.log('import smoke ok'); })()"`
- `pnpm exec tsx -e "import fs from 'node:fs'; import os from 'node:os'; import path from 'node:path'; import { readClaudeCliCredentialsCached, resetCliCredentialCachesForTest } from './src/agents/cli-credentials.ts'; const home=fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(),'openclaw-helper-smoke-')); fs.mkdirSync(path.join(home,'.claude'),{recursive:true}); fs.writeFileSync(path.join(home,'.claude','settings.json'), JSON.stringify({apiKeyHelper:'printf token'})); const cred=readClaudeCliCredentialsCached({homeDir:home, platform:'linux', allowKeychainPrompt:false, ttlMs:0, execSync:()=>{ throw new Error('should not execute'); }}); console.log(JSON.stringify(cred)); resetCliCredentialCachesForTest(); fs.rmSync(home,{recursive:true,force:true});"` -> `{"type":"api_key_helper","provider":"anthropic"}`

Attempted but did not complete in this Windows worktree:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts extensions/anthropic/index.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts --reporter=verbose` stopped after no output for more than 2 minutes.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts --reporter=verbose` stopped after no output for more than 1 minute.
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1` stopped after several minutes in repeated rolldown plugin timing output without test results.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` stopped after more than 2 minutes with no progress beyond the startup line.

target.title: fix(claude-cli): treat apiKeyHelper in settings.json as valid auth
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When Claude CLI is authenticated via `apiKeyHelper` in `~/.claude/settings.json` (the documented mechanism for corporate gateways and dynamic/proxy auth), OpenClaw's pre-spawn auth-gate returns `missing-provider-auth` (`No API key found for provider "anthropic"`), even though the Claude CLI itself authenticates and runs correctly.

The root cause: `readClaudeCliCredentials` / `readClaudeCliCredentialsCached` only reads macOS keychain or `~/.claude/.credentials.json`. There is no path for `apiKeyHelper`. When neither source has credentials, `resolveClaudeCliSyntheticAuth()` returns `undefined` — and both the provider-discovery descriptor and the runtime registration short-circuit before Claude is ever spawned.

This patch adds `hasClaudeCliApiKeyHelper()` to `cli-auth-seam.ts` (same owner boundary, same file-FS injection pattern as `extensions/google/oauth.settings.ts`). Both `resolveClaudeCliSyntheticAuth` functions now fall through to check `apiKeyHelper` when no stored credential exists. If present, they return a sentinel auth result (`CLAUDE_CLI_API_KEY_HELPER_MARKER`) that passes the gate — the actual key is still fetched by the Claude CLI helper script at spawn time, so no real secret ever touches OpenClaw.

Fixes #97489.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed:** Linux users (or any host without `~/.claude/.credentials.json`) who use `apiKeyHelper` for proxy auth get `missing-provider-auth` before Claude CLI is spawned, blocking all claude-cli model routes.

**Real environment tested:** macOS local source checkout, Node v22.22.0, git `0f5aee0d16`, after `pnpm build` (runtime plugin loader reads built dist).

**Repro HOME shape (matches issue):**

```bash
# isolated HOME, deliberately NO ~/.claude/.credentials.json
mkdir -p "$HOME/.claude" "$HOME/bin"
cat > "$HOME/bin/get-anthropic-key.sh" <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
printf '%s' "sk-ant-api03-proof-key-from-apiKeyHelper"
EOF
chmod +x "$HOME/bin/get-anthropic-key.sh"
cat > "$HOME/.claude/settings.json" <<EOF
{"apiKeyHelper":"$HOME/bin/get-anthropic-key.sh"}
EOF
unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_OLD CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
```

**Exact commands run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm build
pnpm exec tsx scripts/proof-claude-cli-api-key-helper-auth.mjs

REPRO_HOME=$(mktemp -d /tmp/openclaw-gateway-proof-XXXXXX)
# ... same HOME setup as above ...
export HOME="$REPRO_HOME"
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_IMAGE_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MCP_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MODEL_SWITCH_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
pnpm test:live src/gateway/gateway-cli-backend.live.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix — auth-gate proof script (`scripts/proof-claude-cli-api-key-helper-auth.mjs`):**

```
[step-1] hasClaudeCliApiKeyHelper()
  ok: settings.json apiKeyHelper detected
[step-2] provider-discovery resolveSyntheticAuth({ provider: claude-cli })
  result: {"apiKey":"claude-cli-api-key-helper","source":"Claude CLI apiKeyHelper","mode":"api-key"}
[step-3] resolveApiKeyForProvider({ provider: claude-cli }) — full auth-gate path
  result: {"apiKey":"claude-cli-api-key-helper","source":"Claude CLI apiKeyHelper","mode":"api-key"}

PASS: apiKeyHelper-only Claude CLI auth passes OpenClaw synthetic auth gate
```

**Evidence after fix — Claude CLI honors apiKeyHelper (direct CLI, same repro HOME):**

```
Failed to authenticate. API Error: 403 {"error":{"type":"forbidden","message":"Request not allowed"}}
```

This is expected with a fake helper key. It proves Claude CLI executed `apiKeyHelper` and reached the upstream API; it did **not** fail with missing local credentials.

**Evidence after fix — Gateway + agent pipeline live smoke (44s runtime, apiKeyHelper-only HOME):**

```
× gateway live (cli backend) > runs the agent pipeline against the local CLI backend 44328ms
  → FailoverError: Request not allowed
```

Log grep for the old failure mode returned **no matches** for `missing-provider-auth`, `No API key`, or `missing.api.key`.

Interpretation: OpenClaw passed the auth gate, spawned Claude CLI via the gateway agent pipeline, and the turn failed only at upstream API auth (403) because the proof helper emits a fake key. That is the correct post-fix failure mode for this repro setup.

**Observed result after fix:** `resolveApiKeyForProvider({ provider: "claude-cli" })` and the gateway CLI-backend live path no longer stop at `missing-provider-auth` when only `apiKeyHelper` is configured.

**What was not tested:** Live success against a real corporate proxy + real helper key (requires operator credentials). The proof intentionally uses a fake helper key to isolate the auth-gate regression without printing secrets.

target.title: feat(session-message-events) socket.drain
target.body:
## Summary

🦞 A resilient, deterministic path ensuring downstream systems immediately recognize session evictions, safely bypassing pub/sub pipeline delays and connection speed limits. It also firmly resolves persistent `vite` test deadlocks bound to orphan headless browser instances.
- **Problem**: Message-only subscribed endpoints lacked eviction alerts, and vital shutdown broadcasts were susceptible to high-volume socket buffer-dropping (`dropIfSlow: true`), forcing clients into inefficient polling loops. Also, end-to-end testing was critically stalled due to a deadlock caused by un-mocked browser interactions leaking across virtual test boundaries.
- **Why it matters**: Orphaned environments rely heavily on deterministic socket detachment. Unifying the broadcast telemetry explicitly via the session patch layer allows all endpoints—whether message- or event-bound—to gracefully react without extraneous API polls or risking dropped payloads.
- **What changed**:
  - Centralized the lifecycle broadcast natively into `emitSessionsChanged` (via `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`), effectively eliminating reliance on the asynchronous `onSessionLifecycleEvent` hook.
  - Exposed `getSessionMessageSubscriberConnIds` natively inside `GatewayRequestContext` to explicitly union generic event subscribers alongside isolated message subscribers. 
  - Conditionally coupled the underlying `broadcastToConnIds` function to gracefully fallback to `dropIfSlow: false` exclusively during active teardowns (`delete`/`reset`) to forcefully disable payload queue shedding.
  - Registered `"socket.drain"` with `[READ_SCOPE]` in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS` (`src/gateway/server-broadcast.ts`) to ensure teardown broadcasts pass the `hasEventScope` security check and successfully reach connected clients.
  - Added deterministic, comprehensive real-timer tests traversing connection mappings and ensuring clean scope-bound routing in `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`.
  - Resolved `vitest` suite deadlocks by explicitly mocking `closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions` in `src/gateway/test-helpers.mocks.ts` to prevent HTTP request leaks against dead CDP instances.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: No existing contracts or `socket.drain` payloads were modified in structure. This is 100% additive logic expanding target scope precision.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63374


## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: 
  1. Added `socket.drain` lifecycle events to websocket `sessions.messages.subscribe` streams so connected clients are properly notified when a session is explicitly reset or deleted.
  2. Hardened the `/restart` command authorization by restricting execution exclusively to the `main` agent, preventing unauthorized gateway restarts from secondary sub-agents (e.g., `tg2`, `tg3`).
  3. Fixed test locking errors and scope issues (`operator.admin`) in the test harness.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS development environment running the OpenClaw gateway (`v2026.5.24`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 
  Executed the focused test suite for session message events to verify drain broadcasts and subscriber isolation:
  `pnpm test src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 
  **Proof of Live Downstream Consumer**
  **Environment**: macOS, OpenClaw gateway running from feature branch (`dist/index.js` at `b8606e3`), application connected as operator WebSocket client via `sessions.subscribe`.
  **Before this patch**: Message-only subscribers (`sessions.messages.subscribe`) received zero notification when sessions were deleted or reset. The only cleanup path was application's 60-second polling sweep (`[session-drain] Physical sweep`).
  **After this patch**: The gateway emits `socket.drain` events on session lifecycle mutations. Application receives them in real-time and flushes immediately.
  **Steps**:
  1. Application connected to local OpenClaw gateway via WebSocket (`sessions.subscribe`)
  2. Orchestration tasks spawned agent sessions (`manager`, `evolver`)
  3. Sessions were deleted during normal lifecycle cleanup
  4. Application received `socket.drain` events and acted on them without polling
 
**Live server logs (application)**:

```
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-outbox-run_5e103304-step_657706f5-r0 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:evolver:autopsy_manager_1779479462764 
[openclaw] Deleted session: agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307 
[session-drain] Database sweep: removed 3 tracked orphaned session(s) 
[openclaw] [CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction: Flushed session agent:manager:c5-run-5e103304-step-8ed9c307
```

**What this proves**: Three sessions were evicted via `socket.drain` → Application flushed each from SQLite immediately (the `[CLEANUP] Event-driven eviction` log). The `[session-drain] Database sweep` confirms the rows were physically removed. Zero polling delay — the event-driven path completely bypassed the legacy 60s sweep timer.
- **Observed result after fix**: All `16` tests passed successfully. The test suite confirms that `socket.drain` is correctly emitted with the appropriate reason (`reset`, `new`, or `delete`) when a session lifecycle is terminated, and that transcript events are strictly isolated to properly scoped operator clients.
- **What was not tested**: Frontend UI (React) receiving `socket.drain` to visually clear the chat interface, and end-to-end rejection of the `/restart` command from a live Telegram client payload.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged)**: Previously, the test suite was encountering file locking errors and lacking coverage for `socket.drain` broadcasts during session resets and deletions. Resolved.


## Root Cause

- **Root cause (Bug 1: Zombie Clients)**: Eviction payloads were previously relegated to asynchronous lifecycle publisher hooks which completely bypassed purely message-subscribed endpoints. Furthermore, even when `socket.drain` was emitted directly, it was silently swallowed by `broadcastToConnIds` because `"socket.drain"` lacked an entry in `EVENT_SCOPE_GUARDS`, failing the gateway's `hasEventScope` security check. Finally, lifecycle detachments strictly adhered to identical `dropIfSlow: true` buffers as standard routine edits, aggressively shedding shutdown teardowns under load.
- **Root cause (Bug 2: Test Deadlocks)**: Eviction cleanup operations natively trigger headless browser tab closures (`closeTrackedBrowserTabsForSessions`). Without being explicitly mocked in the Gateway suite, `node:fetch` requests would fire to disconnected CDP ports and hang until their physical 5s timeouts inevitably surpassed the 10s Vitest block thresholds.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: Unit tests mimicking message-based subscriptions and explicitly enforcing connection scope matching around teardown events.
- **Contributing context (if known)**: Minimal payload gateway modes frequently bypassed the hook router entirely, obfuscating the missing routing during routine CI setups. 

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level that should have caught this**:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: Validate that explicitly isolated `session.message` subscriptions successfully ingest `socket.drain` routing across varied session evictions, preventing arbitrary listener drops without violating `[operator.admin]` permission structures.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: Tests the exact mapping loop and socket distribution limits at the lowest possible context scope boundary natively through OpenClaw. 
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any)**: N/A
- **If no new test is added, why not**: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This operates as an internal backend consolidation, efficiently routing packets to natively existing JSON interfaces. Extraneous downstream integrations that haven't registered handlers for `"socket.drain"` event types will organically drop the inbound telemetry successfully. 

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Base Node
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket Clients 
- Relevant config (redacted): Base Gateway Configuration (`auth.mode = "none"/"token"`)

### Steps

1. Attach an operator to the target `sessions.messages.subscribe` endpoint. 
2. Actively execute `sessions.delete` or `sessions.reset` natively as an `operator.admin` against the tracked target.
3. Validate deterministic receipt of the `socket.drain` telemetry sequence via the local connection buffer. 

### Expected

- `socket.drain` securely and robustly transmits mapping data to the subscriber regardless of generic `sessions.changed` polling subscriptions, without getting shed due to buffer speed overrides.

### Actual

- The telemetry payload flows beautifully without throwing. 

## Evidence

Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Ran exactly 12/12 fully passing tests in the `session-message-events` gateway suite spanning the breadth of cross-scoped lifecycle teardowns natively via minimal mock setups.
- Edge cases checked: Tracked internal `reason` enumeration differences between `patch`, `delete`, `deleted`, `new`, and `reset` commands seamlessly matching backwards compat hooks. 
- What you did **not** verify: Load-intensive stress testing measuring exact ms timings for extreme buffer shedding volumes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: Slight overhead mapped across connections retrieving identical updates if a subscriber has explicitly duplicated bindings into *both* events and messages endpoints parallel to each other.
  - **Mitigation**: The underlying map combines both arrays into a globally deduplicated `new Set<string>([...evSubs, ...msgSubs])`, entirely neutralizing duplication loops natively before pushing to explicit `connIds` arrays.

target.title: fix(agents): attach read tool images to replies
target.body:
## Summary

Persist trusted `read` tool image content into the inbound media store when the tool result has image blocks but no `MEDIA:` path or `details.path`. Queue the saved `media://inbound/...` reference for the next assistant reply so shared outbound delivery — WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, Discord, Slack, Teams, etc. — receives media URLs instead of text-only replies.

Fixes #81322

## Changes

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.ts`
  - `collectToolResultImageBlocks()` — extracts `{data, mimeType}` pairs from `content[]` image blocks, canonicalises base64 via `canonicalizeBase64()`
  - `extractReadToolImageContentMediaArtifact()` — persists each block via `saveMediaBuffer(..., "inbound")` and returns `{ mediaUrls, trustedLocalMedia: true }`; guards: tool must be `read`, must be a builtin, no existing media artifact
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`
  - `emitToolResultOutput()` now falls through to `extractReadToolImageContentMediaArtifact()` when `extractToolResultMediaArtifact()` returns `undefined`; errors are swallowed with a warn log so reply delivery is never blocked
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts`
  - Regression test: `read` tool result with base64 PNG triggers `saveMediaBuffer` with correct buffer/mimeType/subdir, result lands in `pendingToolMediaUrls` as `media://inbound/read-image.png`, and `pendingToolTrustedLocalMedia` is set

## Changes from original PR #81415

Rebased onto current `main` and transplanted from the removed `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.*` file structure to the current `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.*` files. The `trustedLocalMediaToolNames` filtering path is preserved. Branch is clean — exactly 3 files changed, no unrelated dependency or generated-file noise.

## Behavioral Proof

**Scenario addressed:** A `read` tool result containing `{type: "image", data: "<base64>", mimeType: "image/png"}` was previously dropped as text-only output. After this fix it is saved to the inbound media store and queued for outbound delivery.

**Test evidence:**

```
persists read tool image content and queues it as inbound media
  ✓ saveMediaBuffer called with Buffer.from("iVBORw0KGgo=", "base64"), "image/png", "inbound"
  ✓ ctx.state.pendingToolMediaUrls === ["media://inbound/read-image.png"]
  ✓ ctx.state.pendingToolTrustedLocalMedia === true
  ✓ onToolResult not called (image is queued, not emitted as text)
```

**Guard conditions verified:**
- Only fires when `toolName === "read"` and it is in `builtinToolNames`
- Short-circuits if `extractToolResultMediaArtifact()` already found a media path
- Empty image blocks list → returns `undefined`, no side effects
- `saveMediaBuffer` failure → caught, logged as `warn`, reply continues without media

## Verification

```bash
# Only 3 files changed, all scoped to agents handler and tools
git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
# src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts | 44 +++
# src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts            | 13 ++-
# src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.ts                     | 67 +++

# Run the media handler tests
pnpm vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts
```

target.title: test(code-mode): fix cross-platform worker URL test
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the `resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl` test to build file URLs with `pathToFileURL()` from `node:url` instead of hardcoded `file:///repo/...` POSIX strings, so the test is correct on all platforms.

## Problem

The existing test hardcodes POSIX-style paths:

```ts
// Before
expect(testing.resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl("file:///repo/dist/agents/code-mode.js").pathname)
  .toBe("/repo/dist/agents/code-mode.worker.js");
```

On Windows, `pathToFileURL("C:\\repo\\dist\\...")` produces `file:///C:/repo/dist/...` — the hardcoded `file:///repo/...` input and `/repo/...` expectation would never match a real Windows path. The test passes on Linux only because the POSIX path root happens to match.

## Fix

```ts
// After — both input and expected built the same way via pathToFileURL
const workerPath = pathToFileURL(path.join(path.sep, "repo", "dist", "agents", "code-mode.worker.js")).pathname;
const stableUrl = pathToFileURL(path.join(path.sep, "repo", "dist", "agents", "code-mode.js")).href;
const hashedUrl = pathToFileURL(path.join(path.sep, "repo", "dist", "selection-abc123.js")).href;

expect(testing.resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl(stableUrl).pathname).toBe(workerPath);
expect(testing.resolveCodeModeWorkerUrl(hashedUrl).pathname).toBe(workerPath);
```

Because both input URLs and expected pathname go through `pathToFileURL`, they are consistent on any platform.

## Behavioral Proof (Linux, Node v22.22.2)

```
Platform: linux  Node: v22.22.2

=== OLD approach ===
Input URL: file:///repo/dist/agents/code-mode.js
Expected .pathname: /repo/dist/agents/code-mode.worker.js
On Linux: PASSES (incidentally correct — POSIX root matches)
On Windows: FAILS — pathToFileURL produces file:///C:/repo/... not file:///repo/...

=== NEW approach (this PR) ===
stableUrl: file:///repo/dist/agents/code-mode.js
hashedUrl: file:///repo/dist/selection-abc123.js
expected workerPath: /repo/dist/agents/code-mode.worker.js
stable resolves to: /repo/dist/agents/code-mode.worker.js  ✅ match: true
hashed resolves to: /repo/dist/agents/code-mode.worker.js  ✅ match: true
```

The new approach uses `pathToFileURL` for both the input and expected value, so they are always consistent regardless of platform path root.

## Changes

```
src/agents/code-mode.test.ts  — 1 file changed, test-only
```
